commit_scope: Make the agent invitation and /come-in page answer what each sibling meeting taught and how it improved shared harmony and health.

commands: make prompt-gate (initial run blocked by completed squash-merge leftover worktrees)
cd api && python3 -m pytest tests/test_agent_invitation.py -q (expected failure before implementation)
git fetch origin main && git rebase --autostash origin/main
local_validation.summary: Focused invitation tests pass after adding sibling_meeting_learning_summary to the API invitation and a visible learning section on /come-in. Spec quality, generated asset guard, production web build, and the full local API/web verifier passed. The first local verifier run failed on an unrelated cold automation_usage latency threshold; the rerun passed with automation_usage at 8.79ms and no failures. Two completed squash-merge leftover worktrees were removed after git cherry showed their patch IDs were already represented in origin/main. Evidence validation, JSONL validation, diff whitespace check, rebase, local PR guard, and stale PR follow-through all passed; PR guard reported ready_for_push=True.

ci_validation.details: Pending push, PR creation, and GitHub check monitoring.

deploy_validation.details: Pending merge to main and public deploy verification.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: A human or agent can ask what each sibling meeting taught and receive a bounded answer for Grok, Gemini, Codex, and Claude, including learning, harmony/health effect, boundary, and next healthier movement.
test_flows: API ASGI flow: GET /api/agent/invitation exposes sibling_meeting_learning_summary records and boundary language.
Web source flow: /come-in includes a visible What each meeting has taught section.
Web build flow: Next.js production build succeeds.
Local runtime flow: managed API and web verifier passes after rerun.

phase_gate.reason: Local validation and pre-commit guard are complete; push, PR CI, and public deploy validation remain pending.
